channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Seattle police officer fired for fatally hitting graduate student with car
item.description: A Seattle police officer who struck and killed a graduate student from India with his vehicle while responding to an overdose call in 2023 has been fired.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands flee as wildfires burn out of control in and around Los Angeles and homes are destroyed
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — California firefighters battled wind-whipped wildfires that tore across the Los Angeles area, destroying homes, clogging roadways as tens of thousands fled and straining resources as officials prepared for the situation to worsen early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Twice-deported illegal immigrant charged in girlfriend's murder: officials
item.description: Utah authorities have arrested Mexican national Nestor Rocha-Aguayo in connection with the murder of his girlfriend, Talia Benward, of Kearns, Utah, around Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court clears government, adoption agency of liability in adoptee's deportation from US
item.description: A South Korean court has cleared the government and an adoption agency of all liability in a lawsuit filed by a Korean man whose traumatic adoption journey led to an abusive childhood in the United States and ultimately his deportation to South Korea after legal troubles.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 5 in Gaza, including 2 babies, Palestinians say
item.description: Palestinian medics say Israeli airstrikes have killed at least five people in the Gaza Strip, including two infants and a woman. One strike hit a home in the central city of Deir al-Balah, killing two men and a woman, according to the Al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital, which received the casualties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Southern US shivers as new winter storm threatens snow for Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — A developing winter storm threatens to drop snow, sleet and freezing rain on parts of Texas, Oklahoma and Arkansas as frigid air that escaped the Arctic plunges temperatures to subfreezing levels in some of the southernmost points of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California governor says Pacific Palisades wildfire has destroyed many structures as winds kick up
item.description: A wildfire whipped up by extreme winds is sweeping through a Los Angeles hillside dotted with celebrity residences.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wildfire burns through LA enclave known for its beaches and celebrity allure
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rimmed to the south by a 3-mile stretch of Pacific beach, and to the north by craggy bluffs and deep, verdant canyons, the seaside enclave of Pacific Palisades has long been a celebrity hideaway and magnet for those drawn to nature’s doorstep not far from the skyline of downtown L
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The polar vortex brings its bitter cold to the Southern US
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The plunging polar vortex brought subfreezing temperatures Tuesday to some of the southernmost points of the U.S., threatening to dump snow on parts of Texas and Oklahoma in coming days and contributing to a power outage in Virginia's capital that made the water unsafe to drink.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, Fox 5

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rare ski patroller strike at largest resort in US causes long lines and closes terrain
item.description: Ski patrollers miffed by wages they say are too low for high living costs have put a wrench in operations at the biggest U.S. ski resort with a rare strike that began over the busy holidays and carried on into the new year's fresh powder.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: Los Angeles wildfire engulfs Pacific Palisades
item.description: The blaze was first reported as a vegetation fire around 10:30 a.m. PT but had spread 200 acres by the next hour due to strong winds.
item.dc:creator: KTLA, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: PALM BEACH, Florida (AP) — Less than two weeks before taking office, President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday took some of his most audacious claims and promises of the transition period and amped them up to new levels.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, AAMER MADHANI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These will be the most competitive housing markets in 2025, Zillow report predicts
item.description: The hottest housing markets this year are predicted to be on the East Coast, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: In Cincinnati, roller rink provides warmth and shelter from nation's Arctic blast
item.description: CINCINNATI (AP) — Tirrdell Byrd sat on the floor strumming his guitar as people milled about Over-The-Rhine Recreation Center, winter coats flung over folding chairs, belongings packed into shopping bags and wheeled totes, the smell of pizza lingering.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sons of Mexican cartel leader 'El Chapo' are in plea negotiations with US, attorneys say
item.description: Two sons of Mexican drug kingpin "El Chapo" are in plea negotiations with the federal government, while Ismael "El Mayo" Zambada, a longtime leader of the Sinaloa cartel, was recently captured in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Lewis, WGN 9

item.title: Man accused of setting woman on fire on Brooklyn subway pleads not guilty
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta has been accused of intentionally setting fire to 57-year-old Debrina Kawam of New Jersey at an NYC subway station on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, Fox 5

item.title: Peter Yarrow of Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: Yarrow, who also co-wrote the group's most enduring song, “Puff the Magic Dragon,” died Tuesday in New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Donations received to fly migrant girl who lost legs on Mexican train and family
item.description: Inside the Humanitarian Respite Center on Monday a family of a little girl who lost her legs on a train coming to the border were preparing to leave the border after an outpour of donations were sent to help the family.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Strong earthquake kills at least 95 people in western China near Mount Everest
item.description: A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble, and killing at least 95 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's inauguration coincides with an unprecedented string of high-stakes security events in DC
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The last time a president was inaugurated, there was a global pandemic and a violent siege at the very spot at the U.S. Capitol where the ceremony takes place.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minneapolis to overhaul police training, use-of-force policies in wake of George Floyd's murder
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council approved a deal to overhaul the city's police training and use-of-force policies over the 2020 killing of George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris calls it a 'good day' for democracy as she oversees peaceful certification of Trump's victory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was no drama on Monday as Kamala Harris oversaw the ceremonial certification of her defeat to Donald Trump. The vice president kept her remarks perfunctory, and she stood with her hands clasped in front of her while the results from each state were read out loud.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minneapolis agrees to overhaul police training and force policies after George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — The Minneapolis City Council on Monday approved an agreement with the federal government to overhaul the city's police training and use-of-force policies in the wake of the murder of George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump to meet with Senate Republicans on Wednesday
item.description: President-elect Trump will be on Capitol Hill on Wednesday at the invitation of Senate Republican leadership, according to three sources familiar with the invitation.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star choked, punched woman before Myrtle Beach arrest, report says
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Winter weather can disrupt. Here's what to do if your flight is canceled
item.description: Winter weather often causes flight delays and cancellations. While airlines can’t control the weather, they are required in the U.S. to provide refunds to customers whose flights are canceled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Drawn to New Orleans' iconic street of celebration, a night of partying becomes a nightmare
item.description: The night, like countless others Bourbon Street has welcomed over the decades, started out ripe for celebration.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4
